source_document: Connecticut SB 1103 (An Act Concerning Artificial Intelligence)
passage_text: The provisions of sections 3 through 7 of this act shall not apply to: (a) Any artificial intelligence system that is designed to detect security incidents, resist deceptive techniques that attempt to compromise the security of an information system, or identify activities that indicate a potential security threat, provided the system is used solely for cybersecurity purposes; (b) Any narrow artificial intelligence system that is designed and used solely for a single task that does not involve the profiling of natural persons or making decisions that produce legal effects concerning natural persons; (c) Any federal government agency or entity acting under the authority of a federal government agency.

threshold_exception.threshold_value: cybersecurity AI, narrow single-task AI, federal government entities
threshold_exception.threshold_condition: AI systems must be used solely for specified exempt purposes
threshold_exception.applies_to_obligation: Sections 3 through 7 deployer and developer obligations

exceptions.description: AI systems designed to detect security incidents, resist deceptive techniques, or identify security threats
Narrow AI systems designed for a single task
exceptions.conditions: does not involve profiling natural persons or making decisions with legal effects on natural persons

exceptions.description: Federal government agencies or entities acting under federal authority

evidence_spans.text: designed to detect security incidents, resist deceptive techniques that attempt to compromise the security of an information system, or identify activities that indicate a potential security threat, provided the system is used solely for cybersecurity purposes
narrow artificial intelligence system that is designed and used solely for a single task that does not involve the profiling of natural persons

ambiguity.interpretation_notes: The distinction between 'narrow AI' and general-purpose AI is not precisely defined. Modern large language models can be fine-tuned for a single task but retain broader capabilities, creating uncertainty about whether they qualify for this exemption.
ambiguity.suggested_clarification: Define objective technical criteria for classifying an AI system as 'narrow,' such as whether the model architecture, training data, or deployment configuration determines scope
